sections.paragraph:
διοίκησις, εως, ἡ administration, management (X.+; OGI 11, 24; 458, 65; pap; Tob 1:21; Jos., Bell. 1, 669, Ant. 15, 68; 355 δ. Καίσαρος; Just., A II, 8) of a political body: of God’s rule of the world (Epict. 3, 5, 8; 10; 4, 1, 100 al.; Philo, Spec. Leg. 4, 187; cp. Herm. Wr. 10, 23; Ar. [Milne 76, 50]; Just., Ath.) τῇ δ. αὐτοῦ at his direction 1 Cl 20:1.—Dg 7:2.—DELG s.v. οἶκο p. 782. Sv.

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 250
